title: HTTP Semantics, Roles, Representations, and Layered Evidence · Go Client Systems
description: A fleet-control CLI calls an inventory service through a cache and gateway, but operators cannot tell whether failures come from naming, transport, status, or decoding. Learners cumulatively produce a Go 1.26 net/http protocol evidence map through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Distinguish client, server, user agent, origin, intermediary, gateway, proxy, and cache roles per exchange. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Separate a resource from the selected representation and its metadata, content, and current validity. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Trace request construction, routing, transport, intermediary handling, response interpretation, and cleanup as distinct stages. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.
